stories.title: Cloud-native fabric for a US fiber broadband leader
stories.problem: Monolithic legacy stack couldn't keep up with multi-year digital-transformation goals. Error handling was bespoke per service; MTTR was slow; tech debt was compounding.

beats.title: Problem
beats.body: A monolithic legacy estate sitting under a multi-year transformation mandate. 75+ services were on the roadmap and every team was inventing its own retry, observability, and error-handling pattern. MTTR drifted higher with each release.

visual.body:
// before
monolith → custom integrations × N → bespoke error handling × N
MTTR: rising
tech-debt: compounding

beats.title: Approach
beats.body: Decoupled the monolith into a cloud-native, event-driven microservices fabric on GCP. Picked Cloud Run + Pub/Sub for the substrate and Apigee X as the front door. Standardized one Resiliency & Observability framework across every service before any team shipped feature work.

visual.body:
// after
Apigee X (front door)
  → 75+ Cloud Run services
  → Pub/Sub event mesh
  → standardized resiliency + observability

beats.title: Insight
beats.body: Set the framework once, reuse it 75 times. Treat retries, circuit-breakers, structured logs, and trace propagation as platform-level concerns, not service-level decisions. Engineering teams stop re-litigating boring problems and ship faster.

beats.title: Result
beats.body: 40% reduction in tech debt, 2x improvement in MTTR, and a reference architecture for event-driven workflows that the firm now uses across the practice. Secured stakeholder buy-in for Phase 2 by demoing 10+ complex API patterns end-to-end.

visual.body:
// outcomes
−40%  tech debt
+2x   MTTR improvement
+10   reference API patterns adopted
+1    multi-year roadmap green-lit

stories.title: Lead-to-Cash data fabric under M&A timeline
stories.problem: Net-new integration landscape needed end-to-end during aggressive M&A cutover. CRM and ERP didn't talk; inventory and order data were eventually-consistent at best.
stories.role: Lead Architect · 20+ developer team across 3 time zones

beats.title: Problem
beats.body: Net-new integration landscape, ground-zero to production, on an M&A clock. 75+ critical integrations across CRM and ERP. Without governance, the team would ship 75 different ways to do the same thing.

visual.body:
// constraint
M&A cutover  →  hard deadline
75 integrations →  no shared pattern
result if untreated → 75 bespoke implementations

beats.title: Approach
beats.body: Designed an API Catalog and governance model up front. Defined the Lead-to-Cash data fabric end-to-end before any code shipped. Picked patterns once: how CRM events publish, how ERP consumes, how inventory state is sourced of truth.

visual.body:
// API catalog
Lead   → Quote → Order → Cash
  ↓       ↓       ↓       ↓
CRM   →  CPQ   →  ERP  → GL
   (governance · contracts · catalog)

beats.title: Insight
beats.body: Real-time inventory visibility for high-volume semiconductor sales isn't an integration nicety — it's the unlock for sales velocity. Make the data fabric a product, not a side-effect of feature work.

visual.body:
// fabric as product
inventory.live →  CRM, CPQ, OPS
latency: sub-second
source of truth: ERP
consumers: every quote-to-cash surface

beats.title: Result
beats.body: 20% reduction in development cycle time, code redundancy eliminated by the governance model, and 5 new hires upskilled to cloud-native standards. Hit the M&A deadline.

visual.body:
// outcomes
−20%  cycle time
−N    code redundancy
+5    engineers upskilled
+1    M&A timeline hit

stories.title: Quote-to-Cash automation with Salesforce CPQ + NetSuite ZAB
stories.problem: Quote-to-cash was manual at the seams. Errors crept in between Salesforce CPQ and NetSuite billing; subscription billing patterns weren't standardized.

beats.title: Problem
beats.body: Manual data entry between Salesforce CPQ and NetSuite ZAB was the bottleneck. Errors compounded downstream into billing. Legacy middleware was hitting performance limits at the wrong time.

visual.body:
// before
CPQ → human → NetSuite
error surface: high
perf: degrading at peak

beats.title: Approach
beats.body: Engineered the Quote-to-Cash pipeline end-to-end. Spearheaded R&D for NetSuite ZAB (Zone Advanced Billing) integration, establishing the firm's first reference benchmark for subscription billing. Re-architected high-volume interfaces in the legacy middleware layer.

beats.title: Insight
beats.body: Subscription billing is a state-machine, not a sequence of API calls. Encode the state explicitly, design for replay, and treat 500 TPS as the design constraint, not the stretch goal.

visual.body:
// design constraints
peak: 500 TPS
p95 latency: < 200ms
replay: idempotent
state: explicit · auditable

beats.title: Result
beats.body: 30% reduction in order processing time, zero manual data-entry errors, sub-200ms p95 latency at 500 TPS peak. The ZAB integration pattern was adopted as the firm-wide standard reference for subscription billing.

visual.body:
// outcomes
−30%  order processing time
+1    firm-wide standard pattern
+500  TPS peak handled
−100% manual errors
